meta.name: WLEIS Emotional Intelligence Scale
meta.fullName: Wong and Law Emotional Intelligence Scale
meta.description: The WLEIS is a 16-item self-report scale that assesses four dimensions of emotional intelligence: Self-Emotion Appraisal (SEA), Others’ Emotion Appraisal (OEA), Use of Emotion (UOE), and Regulation of Emotion (ROE). It is a concise and reliable measure widely used in organizational behavior and psychology research.
meta.author: Wong & Law (2002)
meta.reference: Wong CS, Law KS. The effects of leader and follower emotional intelligence on performance and attitude: an exploratory study. Leadersh Q. 2002;13(3):243-274.
meta.instruction: Please rate each statement based on your true feelings.

questions.text: I have a good sense of why I have certain feelings most of the time
I have good understanding of my own emotions
I really understand what I feel
I always know whether or not I am happy
I always know my friends’ emotions from their behavior
I am a good observer of others’ emotions
I am sensitive to the feelings and emotions of others
I have good understanding of the emotions of people around me
I always set goals for myself and then try my best to achieve them
I always tell myself I am a competent person
I would always encourage myself to try my best
I am able to control my temper and handle difficulties rationally
I am quite capable of controlling my own emotions
I can always calm down quickly when I am very angry
I have good control of my own emotions

ranges.label: Low EI
ranges.description: Your emotional intelligence score is low. You may have difficulty identifying, understanding, and managing emotions. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Learn to identify and name your emotions
Practice observing others’ emotional cues
Develop self-motivation habits
Consider EI training or psychological counseling
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible

ranges.label: Moderate EI
ranges.description: Your emotional intelligence is at a moderate level. There is room for improvement in certain EI dimensions. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Review subscale scores and target areas for improvement
Practice positive self-talk and emotion regulation
Pay more attention to others’ emotional expressions
Set goals and practice self-motivation
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: High EI
ranges.description: Your emotional intelligence is high. You can understand and manage your own and others’ emotions well. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining strong emotional management skills
Use your EI to enhance relationship quality
Exercise emotional leadership at work and in life
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

ranges.label: Very High EI
ranges.description: Your emotional intelligence is very high. You excel in emotion recognition, understanding, utilization, and regulation. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Your high EI is a valuable social and professional resource
Help others improve their emotional management skills
Serve as an emotional guide in team settings
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

sea.label: Low
sea.description: Low self-emotion appraisal ability. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

sea.label: Moderate
sea.description: Moderate self-emotion appraisal ability. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

sea.label: High
sea.description: High self-emotion appraisal ability. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

sea.label: Very High
sea.description: Very high self-emotion appraisal ability. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support
